text: (I) The Cambrian explosion, which occurred approximately 541 million years ago, marks a pivotal event in the history of life on Earth. (II) During this relatively brief period, the majority of major animal phyla appeared in the fossil record. (III) Prior to this event, life on Earth was mostly composed of simple, single-celled organisms and a few multicellular forms. (IV) Modern birds evolved from a group of two-legged dinosaurs known as theropods, whose members include the fearsome Tyrannosaurus rex. (V) The sudden emergence of such complex and diverse body plans during the Cambrian period continues to puzzle evolutionary biologists.

options.text: I
II
III
IV
V

explanation: Parçada (I), (II), (III) ve (V). cümleler Kambriyen patlamasından ve bu dönemdeki canlı çeşitliliğinden bahsederken, (IV). cümle modern kuşların dinozorlardan evrimi hakkında ilgisiz bir bilgi vermekte ve anlam akışını bozmaktadır.

text: (I) Dark matter constitutes roughly 27 percent of the universe, yet its exact nature remains one of the greatest mysteries in modern astrophysics. (II) Black holes are regions of spacetime where gravity is so strong that nothing, not even light, can escape from them. (III) It does not interact with the electromagnetic force, meaning it does not absorb, reflect, or emit light. (IV) Its presence is inferred solely from its gravitational effects on visible matter, radiation, and the large-scale structure of the universe. (V) Despite numerous underground experiments designed to detect hypothetical dark matter particles directly, scientists have not yet found any conclusive evidence of their existence.

options.text: I
II
III
IV
V

explanation: Metnin geneli (I, III, IV, V) karanlık maddenin özelliklerini ve astrofizikteki önemini anlatırken, (II). cümle kara delikler hakkında bilgi vererek paragrafın konu bütünlüğünü bozmaktadır.

text: (I) Graphene, a single layer of carbon atoms arranged in a two-dimensional honeycomb lattice, has remarkable physical properties. (II) It is incredibly strong, about 200 times stronger than steel, while being exceptionally lightweight and flexible. (III) The process of vulcanization involves adding sulfur to rubber to make it more durable and resistant to temperature fluctuations. (IV) Moreover, its excellent thermal and electrical conductivity makes it a highly promising material for next-generation electronics. (V) Despite these extraordinary characteristics, the mass production of high-quality graphene at an industrial scale remains a significant technological challenge.

options.text: I
II
III
IV
V

explanation: Paragrafın ana konusu olan 'grafen' materyalinin özellikleri I, II, IV ve V. cümlelerde işlenirken, III. cümlede kauçuğun vulkanizasyon işleminden bahsedilmesi anlam bütünlüğünü bozmaktadır.

text: (I) Plate tectonics is the scientific theory that describes the large-scale motions of Earth's lithosphere over millions of years. (II) The lithosphere is broken down into several rigid tectonic plates that glide over the fluid-like asthenosphere beneath them. (III) Interactions at the boundaries of these plates are responsible for phenomena such as earthquakes, volcanic activity, and mountain building. (IV) For example, the Himalayas were formed by the ongoing collision between the Indian Plate and the Eurasian Plate. (V) The Earth's magnetic field is generated by electric currents in the conductive material of its core, created by convection currents due to heat escaping from the core.

options.text: I
II
III
IV
V

explanation: Paragraf (I, II, III, IV), levha tektoniği teorisini ve Dünya'nın litosferik levhalarının hareketlerini anlatmaktadır. Ancak V. cümle Dünya'nın manyetik alanının oluşumuna odaklanarak konu dışına çıkmaktadır.

text: (I) Penicillin, discovered by Alexander Fleming in 1928, was the first true antibiotic and marked a turning point in human history. (II) CRISPR-Cas9 is a revolutionary genome-editing tool that allows scientists to alter DNA sequences and modify gene function. (III) The technology is adapted from the natural defense mechanisms of bacteria and archaea, which use CRISPR-derived RNA and various Cas proteins to foil attacks by viruses. (IV) By delivering the Cas9 nuclease complexed with a synthetic guide RNA into a cell, researchers can cut the cell's genome at a desired location. (V) This precise targeting ability holds immense potential for treating genetic disorders, improving crop resilience, and creating novel biofuels.

options.text: I
II
III
IV
V

explanation: Metinde II, III, IV ve V. cümleler CRISPR-Cas9 gen düzenleme teknolojisini ve nasıl çalıştığını açıklamaktadır. I. cümle ise penisilin keşfiyle ilgili olup paragrafın temasıyla tamamen alakasızdır.

text: (I) The second law of thermodynamics states that the total entropy of an isolated system can never decrease over time. (II) Quantum entanglement is a physical phenomenon that occurs when a group of particles is generated in such a way that the quantum state of each particle cannot be described independently. (III) Entropy is commonly understood as a measure of the disorder or randomness within a closed physical system. (IV) This fundamental principle explains why heat spontaneously flows from a hotter body to a colder one, but never the reverse. (V) Consequently, any natural process will inevitably result in an increase in the overall entropy of the universe, dictating the one-way direction of time.

options.text: I
II
III
IV
V

explanation: Parçada termodinamiğin ikinci yasası ve entropi kavramı işlenmektedir (I, III, IV, V). Ancak II. cümle kuantum dolanıklığı kavramından bahsederek fizik alanında farklı bir konuya geçiş yapmakta ve akışı bozmaktadır.

text: (I) Coral reefs, often referred to as the rainforests of the sea, are some of the most diverse and valuable ecosystems on Earth. (II) They support thousands of species of fish, invertebrates, and marine plants, providing crucial breeding and feeding grounds. (III) Mangrove forests act as crucial coastal buffers, protecting shorelines from severe storm surges and coastal erosion. (IV) However, rising sea temperatures due to climate change are causing widespread coral bleaching, threatening the survival of these delicate structures. (V) If immediate global action is not taken to reduce greenhouse gas emissions, scientists warn that the majority of the world's coral reefs could disappear by the end of the century.

options.text: I
II
III
IV
V

explanation: Paragrafın bütünü mercan resiflerinin (coral reefs) önemi ve iklim değişikliği nedeniyle karşı karşıya kaldıkları tehlikeler üzerine kuruluyken (I, II, IV, V), III. cümlede mangrov ormanlarının (mangrove forests) kıyı koruyucu işlevinden bahsedilmesi konudan sapıldığını gösterir.

text: (I) Enzymes are highly specific biological catalysts that accelerate chemical reactions within living organisms without being consumed in the process. (II) They achieve this by lowering the activation energy required for the reaction to proceed, thereby drastically increasing the reaction rate. (III) The three-dimensional structure of an enzyme is critical for its function, as its active site must precisely fit the substrate molecule. (IV) Synthetic polymers, such as plastics, are manufactured from petrochemicals and take hundreds of years to degrade in natural environments. (V) Any alteration in temperature or pH can denature the enzyme, changing its shape and rendering it completely inactive.

options.text: I
II
III
IV
V

explanation: I, II, III ve V. cümleler enzimlerin biyolojik katalizör olarak nasıl çalıştığını ve yapısal özelliklerini anlatmaktadır. IV. cümle sentetik polimerler (plastikler) hakkında olup paragrafın biyokimyasal temasına uymamaktadır.

text: (I) Nitrogen fixation is a crucial biological process in which atmospheric nitrogen is converted into ammonia or related nitrogenous compounds. (II) Since plants cannot utilize gaseous nitrogen directly from the air, they rely entirely on microorganisms to perform this conversion. (III) Certain bacteria, such as Rhizobium, form symbiotic relationships with the roots of leguminous plants to facilitate this nitrogen exchange. (IV) This mutually beneficial relationship allows the plant to thrive in nitrogen-poor soils while providing the bacteria with essential carbohydrates. (V) Photosynthesis occurs primarily in the chloroplasts of plant cells, utilizing sunlight to synthesize glucose from carbon dioxide and water.

options.text: I
II
III
IV
V

explanation: Paragraf genel olarak bitkiler ve bakteriler arasındaki azot fiksasyonu ve simbiyotik ilişkiyi ele almaktadır (I, II, III, IV). Ancak V. cümlede fotosentez sürecine değinilmiş ve anlatımın odak noktası değişmiştir.

text: (I) The aurora borealis is a natural light display in the Earth's sky, predominantly seen in high-latitude regions, caused by the collision of solar wind and magnetospheric charged particles. (II) El Niño is a climate pattern characterized by the unusual warming of surface waters in the eastern tropical Pacific Ocean. (III) This phenomenon significantly alters global atmospheric circulation, leading to severe weather disruptions across the planet. (IV) For instance, it often causes heavy rainfall and flooding in South America while triggering severe droughts in Australia and Southeast Asia. (V) Predicting the onset and intensity of El Niño events is vital for mitigating their devastating impacts on agriculture, fisheries, and global economies.

options.text: I
II
III
IV
V

explanation: Metnin II, III, IV ve V. cümlelerinde El Niño hava olayı ve küresel etkilerinden bahsedilmektedir. I. cümle ise kuzey ışıklarının (aurora borealis) nasıl oluştuğunu açıklayarak paragrafın bağlamından tamamen kopmaktadır.

context: Nanotechnology, the manipulation of matter on an atomic and molecular scale, is rapidly transforming the field of medicine. By engineering materials at the nanoscale—typically between 1 and 100 nanometers—scientists can create novel therapeutic and diagnostic tools with unprecedented precision. One of the most promising applications is in targeted drug delivery. Conventional chemotherapy for cancer treatment often floods the entire body with toxic drugs, resulting in severe side effects because healthy cells are damaged alongside cancerous ones. In contrast, nanoparticle-based delivery systems can be designed to encapsulate the drug and transport it directly to the tumor site. These nanoparticles can be coated with specific antibodies or ligands that recognize and bind only to receptors found on the surface of cancer cells. Once attached, the nanoparticles release their payload directly into the malignant cells, maximizing the therapeutic effect while minimizing systemic toxicity. Furthermore, nanotechnology is revolutionizing diagnostic imaging. Nanoparticle contrast agents can significantly enhance the resolution of MRI and CT scans, allowing for the detection of tumors at a much earlier stage than is currently possible. Despite these exciting advancements, the widespread clinical adoption of nanomedicine faces several hurdles. The long-term toxicity and environmental impact of various nanomaterials are not yet fully understood, necessitating rigorous safety evaluations and standardized regulatory frameworks before they can be routinely used in human patients.
text: According to the passage, the primary advantage of using nanotechnology in cancer treatment is that it _____.

options.text: eliminates the need for any chemical drugs during the treatment process
enhances the body's natural immune system to fight off cancer cells more effectively
allows for the direct delivery of drugs to cancer cells, reducing damage to healthy tissue
completely removes the tumors without the need for surgical intervention
is significantly cheaper to produce than conventional chemotherapy drugs

explanation: Parçada 'nanoparticle-based delivery systems can be designed to encapsulate the drug and transport it directly to the tumor site... minimizing systemic toxicity' ifadesinden de anlaşılacağı üzere, bu teknolojinin en büyük avantajı ilaçları doğrudan kanserli hücrelere iletip sağlıklı dokulara verilen zararı azaltmasıdır.

text: The passage indicates that nanoparticles can be designed to target cancer cells specifically by _____.

options.text: altering the genetic makeup of the malignant cells to stop their reproduction
being coated with molecules that exclusively attach to receptors on cancer cells
emitting high-frequency radiation that destroys the tumor from within
blocking the blood supply to the tumor, effectively starving the cancer cells
breaking down the cellular membranes of all rapidly dividing cells in the body

explanation: Parçada geçen 'These nanoparticles can be coated with specific antibodies or ligands that recognize and bind only to receptors found on the surface of cancer cells' cümlesine göre, nanopartiküllerin kanser hücrelerinin yüzeyindeki reseptörlere bağlanan moleküllerle kaplanması spesifik hedeflemeyi sağlar.

text: What does the author mention as a benefit of nanotechnology in diagnostic imaging?

options.text: It makes the MRI and CT scanning equipment more affordable for small clinics.
It eliminates the need for patients to be exposed to any form of radiation.
It allows doctors to perform surgical procedures entirely guided by imaging.
It improves the clarity of scans, enabling the detection of tumors in their initial stages.
It can instantly analyze scan results without the need for a radiologist.

explanation: Metinde geçen 'Nanoparticle contrast agents can significantly enhance the resolution of MRI and CT scans, allowing for the detection of tumors at a much earlier stage' ifadesi görüntüleme netliğini (çözünürlüğünü) artırdığı ve böylece tümörlerin erken teşhisini sağladığı bilgisini vermektedir.

text: According to the passage, which of the following is a significant challenge to the widespread use of nanomedicine?

options.text: The manufacturing process for nanoparticles is currently too slow to meet global demand.
There is a lack of comprehensive understanding regarding the long-term safety of nanomaterials.
Most patients' immune systems immediately reject the nanoparticles upon injection.
The pharmaceutical industry is hesitant to invest in unproven nanotechnologies.
Nanoparticles are too unstable to be stored for extended periods in typical hospital environments.

explanation: Parçada yer alan 'The long-term toxicity and environmental impact of various nanomaterials are not yet fully understood' ifadesi uzun vadeli güvenliklerinin tam olarak bilinmemesinin en büyük engel olduğunu göstermektedir.

context: For centuries, scientists viewed forests primarily as collections of independent trees competing for resources such as sunlight, water, and nutrients. However, recent ecological research has fundamentally shifted this perspective by highlighting the critical role of mycorrhizal networks. These intricate, underground fungal networks form symbiotic relationships with the roots of most forest trees. The fungi absorb water and essential minerals, particularly phosphorus and nitrogen, from the soil and transfer them to the trees. In exchange, the trees provide the fungi with carbohydrates produced through photosynthesis, which the fungi cannot manufacture themselves. Beyond simple resource exchange, these networks—often dubbed the 'Wood Wide Web'—facilitate complex communication and resource sharing among trees of different species. Through the fungal pathways, an older, established 'hub tree' can transfer excess carbon and nutrients to younger seedlings growing in shaded areas, significantly increasing their chances of survival. Furthermore, if a tree is attacked by insects or pathogens, it can send chemical warning signals through the network, prompting neighboring trees to upregulate their defensive enzymes before the threat reaches them. While this interconnectedness enhances the resilience of the forest ecosystem as a whole, it also introduces vulnerabilities; the disruption of these fungal networks by logging, soil compaction, or severe droughts can have cascading negative effects on the entire forest community.
text: The passage primarily focuses on _____.

options.text: the competitive nature of different tree species within dense forest ecosystems
the biochemical process of photosynthesis in older, established forest trees
the symbiotic relationships and communication facilitated by underground fungal networks
the devastating impact of logging and soil compaction on forest biodiversity
the various diseases and pathogens that threaten the survival of young seedlings

explanation: Metnin tamamı yer altı mantar ağlarının (mycorrhizal networks) orman ekosistemlerinde ağaçlar arası besin alışverişini ve iletişimi nasıl sağladığına odaklanmıştır.

context: For centuries, scientists viewed forests primarily as collections of independent trees competing for resources such as sunlight, water, and nutrients. These intricate, underground fungal networks form symbiotic relationships with the roots of most forest trees. The fungi absorb water and essential minerals, particularly phosphorus and nitrogen, from the soil and transfer them to the trees. In exchange, the trees provide the fungi with carbohydrates produced through photosynthesis, which the fungi cannot manufacture themselves. Beyond simple resource exchange, these networks—often dubbed the 'Wood Wide Web'—facilitate complex communication and resource sharing among trees of different species. Through the fungal pathways, an older, established 'hub tree' can transfer excess carbon and nutrients to younger seedlings growing in shaded areas, significantly increasing their chances of survival. Furthermore, if a tree is attacked by insects or pathogens, it can send chemical warning signals through the network, prompting neighboring trees to upregulate their defensive enzymes before the threat reaches them. While this interconnectedness enhances the resilience of the forest ecosystem as a whole, it also introduces vulnerabilities; the disruption of these fungal networks by logging, soil compaction, or severe droughts can have cascading negative effects on the entire forest community.
text: According to the passage, how do established 'hub trees' assist younger seedlings?

options.text: By physically shielding them from extreme weather conditions and harsh sunlight
By transferring surplus nutrients and carbon to them via the mycorrhizal networks
By releasing toxic chemicals into the soil to eliminate competing plant species
By altering the soil pH to make it more suitable for the seedlings' root development
By attracting beneficial insects that protect the seedlings from herbivore attacks

explanation: Parçadaki 'Through the fungal pathways, an older, established hub tree can transfer excess carbon and nutrients to younger seedlings' ifadesi yaşlı ağaçların mantar ağları yoluyla genç fidanlara karbon ve besin göndererek yardımcı olduklarını açıkça belirtmektedir.

context: For centuries, scientists viewed forests primarily as collections of independent trees competing for resources such as sunlight, water, and nutrients. Beyond simple resource exchange, these networks—often dubbed the 'Wood Wide Web'—facilitate complex communication and resource sharing among trees of different species. Through the fungal pathways, an older, established 'hub tree' can transfer excess carbon and nutrients to younger seedlings growing in shaded areas, significantly increasing their chances of survival. text: It can be inferred from the passage that the 'Wood Wide Web' _____.

options.text: makes the forest completely immune to all forms of insect and pathogen attacks
is an artificial system implemented by conservationists to restore damaged forests
only connects trees of the exact same species within a given forest area
plays a vital role in forest resilience but can be severely damaged by human activities
extracts nutrients from trees without providing any significant benefits in return

explanation: Son cümlede geçen 'While this interconnectedness enhances the resilience of the forest ecosystem... the disruption of these fungal networks by logging, soil compaction... can have cascading negative effects' ifadesi bu ağların orman direnci için çok önemli olduğunu ancak insan faaliyetleriyle zarar görebileceğini anlatır.

context: Ocean acidification, often referred to as the 'other CO2 problem', is a direct consequence of the increasing levels of carbon dioxide in the Earth's atmosphere. The world's oceans act as a massive carbon sink, absorbing approximately 30 percent of the CO2 released by human activities, such as the burning of fossil fuels and deforestation. When CO2 dissolves in seawater, it forms carbonic acid, which subsequently dissociates into bicarbonate and hydrogen ions. This sudden influx of hydrogen ions lowers the pH of the ocean, making it more acidic. One of the most immediate and devastating impacts of ocean acidification is the reduction of carbonate ions, which are essential building blocks for calcifying marine organisms. Creatures such as coral reefs, oysters, clams, and certain species of plankton rely on these carbonate ions to construct and maintain their calcium carbonate shells and skeletons. As the ocean becomes more acidic, these vital structures not only become more difficult to build but can actually begin to dissolve in the corrosive water. Because these calcifying organisms form the foundation of many marine food webs, their decline could lead to catastrophic collapses of entire marine ecosystems, severely impacting global fisheries and the millions of people who depend on the ocean for their livelihoods.
text: What is the primary cause of ocean acidification according to the passage?

options.text: The dumping of industrial acidic waste directly into coastal waters
The overfishing of calcifying organisms like oysters and clams
The absorption of excess atmospheric carbon dioxide by seawater
The natural volcanic activity occurring deep within the ocean floor
The rapid melting of polar ice caps diluting the ocean's salt concentration

explanation: Parçada 'ocean acidification... is a direct consequence of the increasing levels of carbon dioxide... absorbing approximately 30 percent of the CO2' ifadelerinden asidifikasyonun temel nedeninin deniz suyu tarafından emilen karbondioksit fazlası olduğu anlaşılmaktadır.

text: Why are carbonate ions crucial for certain marine organisms?

options.text: They provide the primary source of metabolic energy for deep-sea creatures.
They are necessary for the organisms to build and maintain their shells and skeletons.
They help regulate the internal body temperature of marine mammals.
They neutralize the toxic chemicals released by harmful algal blooms.
They enable marine plants to perform photosynthesis more efficiently.

explanation: Parçadaki 'carbonate ions, which are essential building blocks... rely on these carbonate ions to construct and maintain their calcium carbonate shells and skeletons' cümlesi karbonat iyonlarının kabuk ve iskelet yapımı için gerekli olduğunu açıklamaktadır.

text: The passage suggests that the decline of calcifying organisms due to ocean acidification _____.

options.text: will likely lead to an overpopulation of non-calcifying predator species
is a temporary issue that the ocean's natural buffering systems will soon correct
only affects isolated regions of the ocean and poses no threat to global fisheries
could trigger the collapse of entire marine ecosystems by disrupting food webs
will force these organisms to rapidly evolve and adapt to freshwater environments

explanation: Son cümlede yer alan 'Because these calcifying organisms form the foundation of many marine food webs, their decline could lead to catastrophic collapses of entire marine ecosystems' ifadesi, bu canlıların kaybının tüm ekosistemin çökmesine yol açabileceğini belirtmektedir.
